expressions.dialogue: Joey: "I had a story all worked out but then **Chandler sold me out**. Well, I'm sorry Joe. I didn't think the doctor was gonna buy that it just *fell* out of the socket."
expressions.speaker: Joey / Chandler
expressions.meaning: 누군가를 배신하다; 상대방에게 불리한 사실을 제3자에게 폭로하다
expressions.nuance: 이익을 위한 배신(betray)보다 가볍고 구어적. 친구 사이에서 반쯤 원망하는 뉘앙스로도 씀.
expressions.example: 파티에 몰래 가려고 했는데 동생이 엄마한테 다 말해버렸어. → I was sneaking off to a party, but my brother sold me out to Mom.

occ.speaker: Rachel
occ.text: I did not sell you out.

occ.speaker: Rachel
occ.text: I did not sell you out.

occ.speaker: Lennart
occ.text: You making fun of me? Because I am not a sell-out. (He stands up and walks menacingly towards Joey) I didn't do that for the money, I believe in those phones. I almost lost a cousin because of bad wireless service.

expressions.dialogue: Joey: "**I had a story all worked out** but then Chandler sold me out."
expressions.speaker: Joey
expressions.meaning: 계획·변명·방법을 미리 완벽하게 마련해 두다
expressions.nuance: "all"을 붙여 완벽하게 준비됐음을 강조. 좋은 계획뿐 아니라 그럴듯한 거짓말을 준비했을 때도 씀.
expressions.example: 면접 전에 예상 질문 답변을 다 준비해뒀는데 처음 보는 질문이 나왔어. → I had all my answers worked out, but they asked something totally unexpected.

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: But hey, listen just so you know, you might have you're work cut out for you. 'Cause when I talked to her, I kinda got the feeling that she's into some other guy. So...

occ.speaker: Ross
occ.text: Oh-oh-okay, but-but I know, that even though I've been a-a complete idiot up 'til now, I mean, I mean you-you-you have to come here. You have to come here so we can work this out.

occ.speaker: Phoebe
occ.text: (feels his arm) Yeah the material feels so soft-hello Mr. Bicep! Have you been working out?

expressions.dialogue: Monica: "**I'm sick and tired of** being depressed about Richard. I needed a plan, a plan to get over my man."
expressions.speaker: Monica
expressions.meaning: ~에 진절머리가 나다, 완전히 지겨워하다
expressions.nuance: 단순한 "tired of"보다 강도가 훨씬 세고 감정적. 오랫동안 쌓인 불만이 폭발 직전일 때 씀.
expressions.example: 맨날 같은 변명만 듣는 거 진짜 지겨워. → I'm sick and tired of hearing the same excuses every time.

occ.speaker: Phoebe
occ.text: Fine! You go learn from your qualified instructor! But don't come crying to me when everyone's sick and tired of hearing you play Bad, Bad Leroy Brown!!

expressions.dialogue: Rachel: "Well, you know, after about thirty or forty fights, you **kinda catch on**."
expressions.speaker: Rachel
expressions.meaning: 경험을 통해 서서히 깨닫다, 요령을 터득하다
expressions.nuance: "figure out"은 단번에 답을 찾는 느낌이지만, "catch on"은 시간이 지나면서 점진적으로 이해하는 뉘앙스.
expressions.example: 처음엔 이 팀 분위기가 어색했는데 몇 주 지나니까 감이 왔어. → I felt awkward at first, but I caught on to the team vibe after a few weeks.

occ.speaker: Ross
occ.text: Hootie and the--oh my. I, I can catch them on the radio.

occ.speaker: Rachel
occ.text: No, I think I'm gonna catch up on my correspondence.

occ.speaker: Phoebe
occ.text: (entering with Rachel) ...and I-I can't take it! Y'know? I'm just, always afraid one of them is gonna catch me with the other one. It's making me crazy.

expressions.dialogue: Rachel: "Okay, **walk us through it**, honey, walk us through it." (after Chandler describes his argument with Janice)
expressions.speaker: Rachel
expressions.meaning: ~에게 과정이나 상황을 단계별로 설명하다, 차근차근 안내하다
expressions.nuance: 복잡한 상황을 처음부터 끝까지 순서대로 설명해달라고 요청할 때. 직장·업무 맥락에서도 자주 씀.
expressions.example: 사고가 어떻게 난 건지 처음부터 순서대로 설명해줄 수 있어? → Can you walk me through what happened, step by step?

occ.speaker: Ross
occ.text: You're joking, right? You guys just walked through the door.

occ.speaker: Gary
occ.text: Oh yeah? Well maybe you and I should take a walk through a bad neighborhood.

expressions.dialogue: Chandler: "Janice likes to cuddle at night, which, you know, **I'm all for**. But when you want to go to sleep, you want some space."
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.meaning: 어떤 것에 완전히 찬성하다, 전적으로 지지하다
expressions.nuance: "I support it"보다 열정적이고 즉각적인 동의. 뒤에 명사·동명사가 오거나 문장 끝에 단독으로도 씀.
expressions.example: 주 4일 근무제? 나 완전 찬성이야. → A four-day work week? I'm all for it.

occ.speaker: Lauren
occ.text: I've been waiting up all night for ya. Where have you been? (Joey doesn't answer) Where have you been? Vic?!

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: (entering) Hey! I'm meeting Kathy in ten minutes! I've been looking all over for you!

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: If marriage worked, I'd be all for it. But do you know what the divorce rate in this country is? 97%.

expressions.dialogue: Ross: "You wait for her to **drift off**, and then you hug her and roll her back over to her side of the bed."
expressions.speaker: Ross
expressions.meaning: 서서히 잠에 빠져들다, 스르르 잠들다
expressions.nuance: "fall asleep"보다 점진적이고 평화로운 이미지. 억지로 잠드는 것이 아니라 자연스럽게 잠드는 느낌.
expressions.example: 아기가 자장가를 들으면서 스르르 잠들었어. → The baby drifted off while listening to the lullaby.

expressions.dialogue: Joey: "Maybe I should call this place and get them to put my 'Days of Our Lives' on here. You know, **juice this puppy up** a little."
expressions.speaker: Joey
expressions.meaning: ~을 더 멋지게·인상적으로 만들다, 매력을 높이다
expressions.nuance: 비격식 슬랭. "puppy"는 특정 사물을 친근하게 부르는 말. 이력서·프레젠테이션 등을 보기 좋게 꾸밀 때 씀.
expressions.example: 이 발표자료 좀 더 눈에 띄게 만들어야 할 것 같아. → We should juice up this presentation to make it stand out more.

expressions.dialogue: Phoebe: "It's partly my fault, 'cause **I made you quit cold turkey**. Sorry, no."
expressions.speaker: Phoebe
expressions.meaning: 나쁜 습관이나 중독을 단번에 완전히 끊다
expressions.nuance: 점진적으로 줄여가는 "taper off"와 반대. 담배·술·약물 외에도 어떤 중독적 행동에도 비유적으로 씀.
expressions.example: 그는 의사 권고를 듣고 커피를 단번에 끊었어. → He quit coffee cold turkey after his doctor's warning.

expressions.dialogue: Phoebe: "I was just afraid that you were still **hung up on** my sister."
expressions.speaker: Phoebe
expressions.meaning: 사람·감정에 집착하다, 감정적으로 매여 있어 헤어나오지 못하다
expressions.nuance: "obsessed"보다 감정적 미련에 더 초점을 둠. 주로 전 연인이나 과거 관계에 집착하는 상태를 표현.
expressions.example: 헤어진 지 2년이 됐는데 아직도 전 남자친구한테서 못 벗어나고 있어. → It's been two years, but she's still hung up on her ex.

occ.speaker: Rachel
occ.text: Which you're not, because you've totally hung up on him!

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: They've only been going out for a few weeks and Phoebe is completely hung up on Mike! She'll say "No", David's heart will be broken, it will be too hard for them to recover from and then Phoebe will end up alone again.

expressions.dialogue: Chandler: "**Wake up and smell the restraining order**, Pheebs."
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.meaning: 현실을 직시해라, 정신 차려
expressions.nuance: 원형은 "wake up and smell the coffee". 여기서는 상황에 맞게 변형해 유머로 씀. 원어민들이 즐겨 쓰는 패턴 변형 위트.
expressions.example: 정신 차려 — 그 사람은 널 이용하고 있는 거야. → Wake up and smell the coffee — he's been using you this whole time.

expressions.dialogue: Phoebe: "Okay, first I'm not crazy. And second, **say it don't spray it**." (after Joey sprayed crumbs while talking)
expressions.speaker: Phoebe
expressions.meaning: 말할 때 침 튀기지 마 (음식 먹으며 말하거나 흥분해서 침을 튀길 때 쓰는 유머 핀잔)
expressions.nuance: 운율이 맞아 기억하기 쉬운 유머러스한 표현. 격식체에선 쓰지 않고 친한 사이에서 가볍게 씀.
expressions.example: 야, 먹으면서 말하지 마 — 침 다 튀잖아. → Hey, I can feel your enthusiasm — say it, don't spray it!

expressions.expression: I asked for the news, not the weather
expressions.dialogue: Phoebe: (after Joey sprays her with crumbs while talking) "**Okay, I asked for the news, not the weather.**"
expressions.speaker: Phoebe
expressions.meaning: 말은 말로 해, 침 튀기지 마 (재치 있는 핀잔)
expressions.nuance: "say it, don't spray it"과 같은 상황에서 더 위트 있게 쓰는 고급 표현. 직접 외워두면 일상에서 써먹을 수 있음.
expressions.example: 신나서 말하다가 침을 튀기자 친구가 웃으며 받아쳤다. → When he spit while talking excitedly, she quipped, "I asked for the news, not the weather."

expressions.dialogue: Monica: "I see the way Ben looks at you. **It makes me ache**, you know?"
expressions.speaker: Monica
expressions.meaning: 가슴이 아리다, 그리움·부러움 등으로 마음이 쓰리다
expressions.nuance: 신체적 통증이 아닌 감정적 갈망이나 슬픔을 표현. "ache for something"도 같은 맥락으로 씀.
expressions.example: 오래된 사진들을 보니 그때가 너무 그리워서 가슴이 아렸어. → Looking at old photos made me ache for those simpler days.

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: Listen, while you're on a roll, if you feel like you gotta make like a Western omelet or something... (Joey and Chandler taste the coffee, grimace, and pour it into a plant pot.) Although actually I'm really not that hungry...

occ.speaker: Mr. Geller
occ.text: C'mon, you'll make a day of it! You'll rent a boat, pack a lunch...

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: But my mom always makes them. It's like a tradition. You get a little piece of turkey on your fork, a little cranberry sauce, and a tot! It's bad enough I can't be with my family because of my disease.

expressions.dialogue: Phoebe: "You just seem to be a really nice guy. **Don't be so hard on yourself**, okay."
expressions.speaker: Phoebe
expressions.meaning: 자책하다, 스스로에게 너무 가혹하게 굴다
expressions.nuance: 자기비판이 지나친 사람을 위로할 때 쓰는 대표적 표현. "give yourself a break"도 비슷한 상황에서 씀.
expressions.example: 한 번 실수한 거잖아. 너무 자책하지 마. → It was just one mistake. Don't be so hard on yourself.

occ.speaker: Phoebe
occ.text: Eh, don't be so hard on yourself. If someone I was still in love with was getting married...

expressions.dialogue: Monica: "I figured out I need to charge seventeen bucks a jar just to **break even**. So, I've got a new plan now."
expressions.speaker: Monica
expressions.meaning: 손익이 본전이 되다, 이익도 손실도 없는 상태가 되다
expressions.nuance: 사업·투자 맥락에서 자주 씀. 일상에서도 "겨우 본전이야"라는 의미로 비유적으로 씀.
expressions.example: 첫 달은 겨우 본전치기 했어. → We barely broke even in the first month.

expressions.dialogue: Monica: "The great thing about the jam plan was, **I was taking control of my life**. So I asked myself, what is the most important thing to me in the world?"
expressions.speaker: Monica
expressions.meaning: 삶의 주도권을 잡다, 스스로 삶을 이끌어나가다
expressions.nuance: 수동적 상태에서 능동적으로 전환하는 순간을 표현. 자기계발 맥락에서 자주 쓰이며 지금도 널리 씀.
expressions.example: 그녀는 회사를 그만두고 직접 삶을 개척하기로 결심했어. → She quit her job and decided to take control of her life.

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: Okay, look, this is probably for the best, y'know? Independence. Taking control of your life.  The whole, 'hat' thing.

expressions.dialogue: Malcolm: "Do you wanna hear something from it?" Phoebe: "Um, **not even a little bit**." Malcolm: "It's about you." Phoebe: "Oh, okay then."
expressions.speaker: Phoebe
expressions.meaning: 전혀 아니야, 조금도 그렇지 않아 (강한 부정)
expressions.nuance: 단순한 "No"보다 단호하고 재치 있는 부정. 완전한 거절이나 무관심을 유머러스하게 표현할 때 씀.
expressions.example: 또 그 영화 보고 싶어? — 조금도 보고 싶지 않아. → "Want to watch that movie again?" "Not even a little bit."

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: (entering) Rachel, I need to borrow-You're not packed!!!! You're not packed even a little bit!

expressions.dialogue: Phoebe: "No, no, no. You know what, **he's not into that stuff anymore**. He quit for me."
expressions.speaker: Phoebe
expressions.meaning: ~에 빠져 있다, ~를 좋아하다·즐기다
expressions.nuance: 취미·사람·활동 등 다양한 대상에 폭넓게 씀. "not into"는 관심이 없거나 그만뒀다는 뜻. 매우 일상적인 구어 표현.
expressions.example: 나 요즘 재즈에 완전 빠졌어. → I've been really into jazz lately.

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: Oh no, it's terrific, it's... it's... y'know, you deserve this, after all your years of struggling, you've finally been able to crack your way into showbusiness.

occ.speaker: Young Ethan
occ.text: Uuh, before we get into any staying-over-stuff, there is something you should know.

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: You know, it's... (sees the feeding taking place next to him) something funny about sneakers. I'll be right back. (Goes into the kitchen)

expressions.dialogue: Monica: "Brown hair, green eyes..." Joey: "**No kiddin', hmm.**"
expressions.speaker: Joey
expressions.meaning: 진짜? 그렇구나 (놀람 혹은 공감); 뻔한 말에 비꼬듯 쓰기도 함
expressions.nuance: ①진심으로 놀랐을 때 쓰기도 하고, ②상대방이 너무 뻔한 말을 할 때 약간 비꼬는 뉘앙스로도 씀. 문맥에 따라 완전히 달라짐.
expressions.example: "서울에 인구가 천만 명 정도 된대." "진짜? 몰랐네." → "Seoul has about ten million people." "No kidding, I had no idea."

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: No, you kidding? The guy's a freak.. (Ross enters off camera)

occ.speaker: Ross
occ.text: No kidding?

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: Nothin', nothin', hey you're a cheapasaurus. I'm kidding, I'm kidding, thank you, I'm very greatful.

expressions.dialogue: Phoebe: "He's just like this very passionate, incredibly romantic guy that **got like a tinsy bit carried away**, you know."
expressions.speaker: Phoebe
expressions.meaning: 흥분이나 감정에 휩쓸려 지나치게 행동하다, 선을 넘을 만큼 열중하다
expressions.nuance: 의도는 좋았지만 정도가 지나쳤을 때 씀. "a little/a bit"을 붙여 완화하거나 변명 뉘앙스로도 자주 씀.
expressions.example: 쇼핑하다가 너무 신나서 필요도 없는 것들까지 다 사버렸어. → I got a little carried away shopping and bought things I didn't even need.

occ.speaker: Ross
occ.text: I'm understanding, but let's not get carried away.

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: (laughs) Now let's not get carried away. (He walks away as Monica comes over and hugs Chandler from behind.)

expressions.dialogue: Rachel: "Oh, all right, well **it's not like I'm employee of the year or anything**." (deciding to be late for work to stay with Ross)
expressions.speaker: Rachel
expressions.meaning: 뭐, ~인 것도 아니잖아 (행동을 가볍게 합리화하거나 자조할 때 씀)
expressions.nuance: 자신의 행동을 정당화하는 구어체 표현. 약간 자조적이거나 체념한 뉘앙스가 담겨 있음.
expressions.example: 뭐, 내가 모범생인 것도 아닌데 하루쯤 결석하면 어때. → It's not like I'm the top student or anything — missing one day won't hurt.

occ.speaker: Rachel
occ.text: What? Come on, it's not like I'm asking for this girly clock or anything, which, by the way, I also think is very cool.

occ.speaker: Phoebe
occ.text: It's not like we-we know each other or anything. Or that have anything in common.

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: You! It's not like it's a big deal! You-you don't still do it or anything!

expressions.dialogue: Ross: "Okay, one question." Chandler: "**Shoot.**"
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.meaning: 어서 말해봐, 질문해봐 (상대방의 말을 재촉할 때 쓰는 간결한 표현)
expressions.nuance: 누군가 "I have a question/favor"라고 운을 떼면 "Shoot."으로 즉시 "말해봐"를 표현. 격식 없는 대화에서 매우 자주 씀.
expressions.example: "저 부탁이 하나 있는데요." "어서 말해봐요." → "I have a favor to ask." "Shoot."

occ.speaker: Mr. Geller
occ.text: Look, there are people like Ross who need to shoot for the stars, with his museum, and his papers getting published. Other people are satisfied with staying where they are- I'm telling you, these are the people who never get cancer.

occ.speaker: All
occ.text: Shoot! Shoot! Shoot!

occ.speaker: Phoebe
occ.text: Yeah, but did they have to shoot him down? I mean, that was just mean.

expressions.dialogue: Joey: "Sounds nice." Monica: "**'Fraid so.** Brown hair, green eyes..."
expressions.speaker: Monica
expressions.meaning: 유감이지만 그래, 안타깝게도 맞아
expressions.nuance: "I'm afraid so"의 구어체 축약형. 상대방이 듣기 싫어할 사실을 부드럽게 인정할 때 씀. 지금도 일상에서 자연스럽게 씀.
expressions.example: "그 식당 벌써 문 닫았어?" "유감이지만 그래, 지난달에 폐업했어." → "Did that restaurant already close down?" "I'm afraid so — it shut last month."

expressions.dialogue: Rachel: "All right, are you guys gonna come down?" Ross: "Uh, yeah, yeah, **I'm right behind you**."
expressions.speaker: Ross
expressions.meaning: 바로 따라갈게, 금방 뒤따라갈게
expressions.nuance: 물리적으로 뒤따라가는 것 외에도 "나도 곧 할게"의 뉘앙스로 씀. 매우 일상적이며 지금도 흔하게 쓰이는 표현.
expressions.example: "먼저 들어가 있어." "알았어, 바로 뒤따라갈게." → "Go on ahead." "I'm right behind you."
